leadText: Behemoth Bash (Japanese: きょじゅうだん Behemoth Impact) is a damage-dealing Steel-type move introduced in Generation VIII. It is the signature move of Zamazenta.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.1.1 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.2 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] Behemoth Bash deals damage. If the target is Dynamaxed or Gigantamaxed , its damage is doubled. The bonus does not apply to Eternamax Eternatus , despite its similarities to a Gigantamaxed Pokémon. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Behemoth Bash cannot be selected in a battle. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] As there is no Dynamax mechanic in Generation IX, Behemoth Bash now deals damage with no secondary effect.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Sw Sh LA The user becomes a gigantic shield and slams into the target. This move deals twice the damage if the target is Dynamaxed. BD SP This move can't be used. It's recommended that this move is forgotten. Once forgotten, this move can't be remembered. S V The user's body becomes a firm shield and slams into the target fiercely.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Behemoth Bash and Behemoth Blade were formerly not present in the mobile version of Pokémon HOME 's move Pokédex , as they are the signature moves of Crowned Shield Zamazenta and Crowned Sword Zacian (which are forms that appear only in battles and, therefore, cannot be deposited into HOME) and cannot be learned by a Smeargle via Sketch , as it is not present in Pokémon Sword and Shield . With the addition of Smeargle to Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , the two moves were finally added to Pokémon HOME . This distinction is shared with Glacial Lance and Astral Barrage for the same reasoning, as well as Struggle and Light of Ruin , both of which cannot be legitimately learned by any Pokémon .
